__comment: EuroTransit USE dashboard — Utilization / Saturation / Errors for the pods behind the money path. Complements the RED board: RED tells you users are hurting, USE tells you which resource is the cause. Also the board to watch during chaos CE-3 (node drain) and rollouts.
title: EuroTransit — USE (infrastructure)

panels.title: CPU utilization — cores per pod
panels.description: U of USE (CPU). Compare against the container requests/limits set in values.yaml.

panels.title: CPU saturation — throttled period ratio
panels.description: S of USE (CPU). Throttling >0 with normal utilization means the CPU LIMIT is the bottleneck, not the node — latency degrades before usage looks high.

panels.title: Memory — working set per pod
panels.description: U of USE (memory). Approaching the limit = OOMKill risk (an involuntary disruption PDBs cannot protect against).

panels.title: Errors — container restarts (10m)
panels.description: E of USE. Restart loops here + healthy RED = liveness misconfiguration; restarts + RED errors = real crashes. The signal to watch during chaos CE-2 (pod kill).

panels.title: Network I/O per pod
panels.description: U of USE (network). Asymmetries between services reveal where the pipeline volume actually flows.

panels.title: Ready replicas per deployment
panels.description: Availability at the replica level. PromQL aggregates by deployment so kube-state-metrics target restarts cannot create multiple identically named history fragments. During CE-3 (node drain) this must never hit 0 for a money-path service — that is the PDB doing its job.
